steps.title: Find the multiplicative factor for each unit increase in w
steps.content:
When $w$ increases by 1, compute the ratio of successive function values:
$$\frac{z(w+1)}{z(w)} = \frac{(0.829)^{2(w+1)}}{(0.829)^{2w}}$$
Since the exponent in the numerator is $2(w+1) = 2w + 2$ and in the denominator is $2w$, the exponent difference is 2:
$$\frac{z(w+1)}{z(w)} = (0.829)^{2}$$
So the function value is multiplied by $(0.829)^2$ whenever $w$ increases by 1.

steps.title: Calculate the percentage decrease
steps.content:
Compute $(0.829)^2$:
$$(0.829)^2 = 0.687241$$
This means $z(w+1) = 0.687241 \cdot z(w)$. The value retains approximately 68.72% of its previous amount.

The percentage decrease is:
$$p = (1 - 0.687241) \times 100\% = 31.28\%$$
This rounds to 31.3%. That matches choice D — D is correct.
